headline: Why New CEOs Aren’t Quick Fixes for Sluggish Stocks
summary: On Monday,  Domino’s Pizza  named Joe Jordan, its operating chief and U.S. president, as its new CEO.  The internal succession didn’t impress investors just yet as slowing sales growth and softer pizza demand came under scrutiny.  Shares fell 5.6% on the announcement day and slipped again the following session.

headline: Pizza Has Gone Cold. Domino’s Is Still Worth a Look.
summary: The category’s sales are stagnant and its chains are faltering, but Domino’s can emerge as a winner
